hero.factFocusBody: SKILL.md, mcp.json, shared Claude settings, Cursor rules, and Copilot instruction files.
hero.factOutputsBody: Stable findings first, Preview for deeper review, plus explicit compatibility and supply-chain lanes when teams want more than the quiet default.

demo.title: lintai flow
demo.subtitle: Scan the config and instruction files that steer agents in your repo.
demo.summaryLabel: Rotating through the strongest repo-local surfaces

index.title: Find the files that steer agents
index.body: Index skills, MCP wiring, hook settings, and instruction files before they quietly become team defaults.

scan.title: Run the clearest checks first
scan.body: Start from stable config and policy signals instead of vague prose-only heuristics.

ship.title: Ship evidence into review or CI
ship.body: Export SARIF or JSON so the same findings stay readable in review, CI, and downstream tooling.

skills.title: Unscoped Bash grant in shared frontmatter
skills.summary: lintai catches the broad shell grant that still shows up too often in shared skills and agent markdown.
skills.remediation: Scope Bash(...) to the exact workflow instead of shipping a broad shell default.

claudeHooks.title: Mutable package launcher in a committed hook
claudeHooks.summary: Shared Claude hooks should be reviewed automation, not runtime package resolution through npx or uvx.
claudeHooks.remediation: Replace the mutable launcher with a vendored script or another reproducible local command.

mcp.title: Mutable runner inside committed mcp.json
mcp.summary: Committed MCP wiring is executable setup. lintai checks that the launch path is reproducible and reviewable.
mcp.remediation: Switch to a pinned local wrapper, vendored script, or another reviewed execution path.

claudePerms.title: Wildcard shell and write access in shared policy
claudePerms.summary: Broad Claude permissions are easy to copy across repos and much harder to defend in review than a narrow allowlist.
claudePerms.remediation: Replace Bash(*) and Write(*) with scoped command patterns and path-specific write targets.

cursor.title: Unknown or contradictory Cursor frontmatter
cursor.summary: lintai treats shared .mdc routing like config, not prose, and catches rule-pack drift before it turns into tribal knowledge.
cursor.remediation: Keep Cursor rules on supported keys only and remove globs from alwaysApply rules.

copilot.title: Invalid applyTo glob in path-specific instructions
copilot.summary: A broken applyTo can look fine in review while silently failing to target the files the team expects.
copilot.remediation: Use valid reviewed glob patterns so the instruction contract stays testable and explicit.

download.quickstartSubtitle: This is the fastest supported path from install to a representative lintai run.
download.supportSubtitle: The honest release view: what the product is aimed at today and where the trust boundary really is.

features.sectionTitle: Security checks built for AI-native repo surfaces
features.sectionSubtitle: lintai is deliberately narrow: it checks the config and instruction files that actually change how AI agents behave.

featuredRules.sectionTitle: Start with the most defendable rules first
featuredRules.sectionSubtitle: These rules cover executable config, shared policy, and editor instructions - the parts of the product that are easiest to explain and defend in real review.

faq.subtitle: The practical version: what lintai scans, where the current v0.1 boundary is, and how teams usually start using it.

quickLinks.title: Need a clearer next step?
quickLinks.subtitle: Most teams only need three pages first: the docs index, the positioning note, and the release contract.
quickLinks.docsBody: Browse the rules and preset reference without leaving the repo context.
quickLinks.rulesBody: See who lintai is for, what it catches, and what it does not promise yet.
quickLinks.releaseBody: Read the current public release posture before promising more than the product ships.

comparison.sectionSubtitle: So risky defaults do not quietly spread, and teams can keep default checks separate from deeper review lanes.

footer.tagline: Offline-first checks for AI agent configs and instructions.

meta.homeTitle: lintai | Fast offline security checks for AI agent artifacts
meta.homeDescription: lintai helps teams verify skills, MCP configs, hook settings, local client policy files, and AI instruction files with deterministic findings, evidence, and CI-ready output.
meta.downloadTitle: Get started with lintai | Install the public release CLI
meta.downloadDescription: Choose the right install path for lintai, open the docs, and start with a fast offline scan of your repository.
